growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron and SK Hynix bounced after Alphabet raised 2026 capex guidance to $195 billion-$205 billion, and Micron's recent fiscal Q3 report showed data center revenue above a $25 billion annualized run rate, making the HBM and DRAM shortage trade look anchored in real demand rather than sentiment alone.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI memory demand, record Q3 results, tight HBM/DRAM supply, and a low forward multiple outweigh cyclical and China competition risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Microsoft has sold off sharply in 2026 after AI-capex concerns and now trades well below its 52-week high, but analysts still see Azure and Copilot as durable growth engines with limited downside at current levels.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft combines quality, positive cash generation, Azure growth, Copilot monetization, institutional ownership, and a near-term earnings catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Sandisk is one of July's biggest tech laggards even after a huge run earlier in 2026, and the market is now pricing in a much more cautious setup despite strong earnings growth and a low forward multiple.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Sandisk combines a low forward multiple with a NAND/AI memory supercycle, huge earnings beats, and near-term earnings plus investor-day catalysts.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom and OpenAI unveiled an LLM-optimized intelligence processor on June 24, 2026, with deployment aimed at gigawatt-scale data centers, while Broadcom's latest quarterly release showed AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion, up 143% year over year, confirming monetization at scale beyond the Nvidia trade.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom has scaled AI semiconductor revenue, custom accelerator partnerships, and strong free cash flow at a still-defensible forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow proved its AI monetization on July 22, 2026 with subscription revenue up about 25% year over year, current RPO at $13.2 billion, and AI annual contract value above $1 billion, while a fresh $40 million banking-software investment extends its AI workflow push into financial services.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow's Q2 beat, raised guide, $1B-plus AI ACV, and deep drawdown create a strong software-quality setup despite AI disruption risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The U.S. Commerce Department said TSMC will add $100 billion more to Arizona investment for a total of $265 billion, deepening the U.S.-Taiwan industrial tie-up just as Beijing considers tighter controls on foreign chipmakers and Chinese use of TSMC services.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI foundry dominance, Q2 margin strength, raised guidance, and pricing power outweigh heavy capex concerns.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Uber showed a very large insider accumulation event in OpenInsider's monthly flow, with a $20M purchase by a 10% owner and another recent multi-million-dollar buy, which is notable for a profitable platform name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Profitable platform growth, major insider accumulation, Delivery Hero optionality, and August earnings create a strong risk/reward setup after a 32% drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Defense is still seeing strong institutional support, and Lockheed just posted a record backlog and a major THAAD contract win while raising its outlook, which keeps the sector rotation case intact.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Record backlog and raised guidance make Lockheed a quality defense compounder, but the post-earnings jump leaves limited near-term upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv expanded its Tognana, Italy campus on July 21, 2026 to double regional chiller capacity for AI-ready cooling, and two days later said it deployed integrated power and liquid cooling for NVIDIA's GB300 system at the Naval Postgraduate School, reinforcing a multi-year AI thermal bottleneck story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv remains a direct bottleneck beneficiary of AI power and cooling demand, though valuation and beta argue for disciplined sizing.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: EPAM Systems is 61% off its $222.53 high at $85.83, still earns $386.7M annually, and reports Aug. 6 after TD Cowen and Wedbush cut targets into the July earnings window, so sentiment is washed out while the AI and cost-optimization story is still intact; bear case is client delays and macro uncertainty keep recovery slow.
growth_picks.analyst_take: EPAM is a washed-out, cash-generative IT-services name with AI revenue momentum and a near-term earnings catalyst, but client delays cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Alphabet was hit hard by the July 23 AI-spend selloff, but the stock still looks like a profitable franchise trading at a much lower multiple than its growth profile suggests.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Alphabet's core growth and cloud AI traction justify buying the selloff, but historic negative free cash flow keeps conviction below high.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Elevance drew a high-signal insider cluster, including CEO Gail Boudreaux buying more than $1.0M of stock and another director purchase over $1.3M, which is the kind of open-market commitment that can matter near sentiment lows.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Elevance offers cheap managed-care exposure with meaningful insider buying, but Medicaid margin pressure prevents a higher-conviction call.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's July 7 Ankara summit said Denmark, Finland, Germany, and Norway will buy up to five MQ-4C Triton aircraft, and NATO's own transcript says Northrop Grumman is building the Triton and supporting the ISR network, creating a fresh allied procurement tailwind.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop's record backlog, raised guidance, and allied ISR/strategic-defense demand make the pullback attractive despite margin pressure.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's rare-earth magnet exports to the U.S. are still about 20% below pre-trade-war levels, and new EU/U.S. critical-mineral controls keep supply tight, which directly supports the only major U.S. rare-earth producer and magnet supply-chain builder.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is speculative but strategically important, with U.S. government and Apple-backed rare-earth magnet catalysts offsetting weak current profitability.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: President and 10% owner Andrew Kai Kang bought 272,405 shares at $36.71 for $9,999,988 on July 14, 2026, and the filing history shows earlier May accumulation by the same insider, making this a rare eight-figure insider conviction buy.
growth_picks.analyst_take: A rare eight-figure insider purchase below recent trading levels creates a speculative robotics/physical-AI setup despite thin fundamentals.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group disclosed a 5,906,542-share purchase in Jefferies at $53.96 per share for $318,717,006, lifting its ownership by 222% and signaling a major strategic institutional stake rather than passive index ownership.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strategic SMBC ownership, improving capital-markets earnings, and a low forward multiple make Jefferies a reasonable financials re-rate candidate.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Intuitive Machines had 37.84 million shares short, equal to 28.85% of float, with about 2.66 days to cover, and it heads into an estimated August 6, 2026 earnings report where revenue is expected to jump to $221.67 million from $50.31 million, creating a classic squeeze-plus-catalyst setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NASA CLPS awards, explosive revenue guidance, and heavy short interest create a credible catalyst trade, but not a quality compounder yet.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: IBM cut revenue guidance and the stock got hit hard, but management kept free cash flow guidance intact and analysts framed the miss as deferred deals rather than a broken thesis.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The historic selloff prices in a lot of execution failure, while free-cash-flow guidance stayed intact and deferred deals may close in H2.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Comcast is a classic contrarian setup: the stock is still far below its 52-week high, trades at a low single-digit multiple, and yields over 6% while investors debate the cable and streaming turnaround.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Comcast is cheap enough for a contrarian buy after Q2 beat expectations, broadband losses narrowed, and Peacock momentum improved.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 10, 2026, EquipmentShare raised 2026 guidance and announced a $500 million buyback program, a strong capital-allocation signal that usually matters most when the market is still figuring out the story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Raised 2026 guidance and a $500 million buyback are strong signals, with valuation reasonable on forward earnings despite balance-sheet risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX is benefiting from the same defense spending rotation as Lockheed, and its updated full-year outlook plus higher backlog suggests the order cycle is still improving rather than peaking.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has excellent backlog momentum but is already trading near highs with a richer forward multiple and little target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Credo's AI connectivity ramp is accelerating: current coverage projects about US$600 million of AI optics revenue, the company already said fiscal 2026 revenue more than tripled to $1.3 billion, and its DustPhotonics acquisition expands the 800G to 3.2T optical stack for hyperscale clusters.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Credo's AI connectivity growth is exceptional, but valuation, customer concentration, and insider selling keep it from being a fresh buy here.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Tractor Supply is 53% off its $63.99 high at $30.32 after the July 23 Q2 print and guidance cut, yet it remains profitable with $1.01B of net income and management is closing Petsense stores, expanding pet services, and reallocating capital, which can turn the miss into a reset; bear case is that the 2026 growth thesis just got weaker and analyst cuts may keep coming.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tractor Supply is a profitable reset story, but the Q2 miss and withdrawn long-term framework mean the turnaround is not yet proven.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Viatris has a near-term binary catalyst with a July 30 PDUFA date for its weekly contraceptive patch, and the calendar suggests a meaningful stock move if the FDA decision lands favorably.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Viatris is cheap and cash-generative with a PDUFA catalyst, but the stock is near highs and consensus upside is too thin for a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: EIA says global LNG trade hit a record and Europe's LNG imports rose sharply, while the EU's latest Russia sanctions restrict LNG carrier sales and keep pressure on Russian cargo routing, a direct tailwind for the largest U.S. LNG exporter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere is a high-quality LNG security beneficiary, but near-high pricing, leverage, and limited target upside argue against adding aggressively.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Red Sea and Hormuz disruptions have pushed refining margins to record levels, and Barron's and WSJ coverage says U.S. refiners are extracting outsized profits from scarce gasoline and diesel supply, with Valero specifically cited as a major beneficiary of the crack-spread surge.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Valero is benefiting from record crack spreads, but the stock is near highs and consensus value is already exhausted.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 20, 2026, CEO Thomas J. Kemly bought 53,057 shares at $10.00 ($530,570), Sr. EVP/COO Steven M. Klein bought 50,000 shares at $10.00 ($500,000), and director Robert Van Dyk bought 25,000 shares at $10.00 ($250,000), a same-day cluster of open-market bank buying that looks like management conviction after weakness.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider cluster buying is real, but valuation and offering mechanics leave limited margin of safety above the $10 deal price.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Redwire's short interest rose to 43.05 million shares, or 26.2% of public float, with only 1.28 days to cover, while Marine Corps Stalker drone orders were expanded, giving the stock a tighter squeeze profile with a real operational catalyst.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense drone orders and short interest are interesting, but severe losses, high beta, and negative FCF keep this below buy quality.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Symbotic is emerging as a physical-AI winner after a July 15 article highlighted Amazon's expanding warehouse robotics spend, and the company also acquired ARMS Innovations on July 2 to broaden AI-driven warehouse optimization beyond fixed automation into software-guided operations.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Warehouse automation demand is real, but the stock still prices in too much perfection for a thin-margin business.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: ARI is 40% below its $11.24 high at $6.76, remains profitable with $112.1M of net income, and heads into Jul. 30 earnings after a Jul. 16 special cash distribution, so any stabilization in commercial real-estate spreads or rates could unlock a re-rate; bear case is CRE credit stress and a still-stretched forward multiple.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The book-value discount and earnings catalyst are offset by mortgage-REIT leverage and unresolved commercial real-estate credit risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Arrowhead has a July 2026 phase 1/2a data readout on the calendar, and the stock is already a large-cap biotech with enough scale that a positive readout could re-rate the name quickly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Plozasiran Phase 3 data are excellent, but the stock has already re-rated near highs while losses and pipeline timing keep risk elevated.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Fox is being priced like a declining media asset even though it remains a profitable large-cap name, and the stock is nearly 28% below its high while a revived M&A backdrop keeps optionality alive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fox is statistically cheap with positive FCF, but negative growth and investor skepticism around media M&A cap the buy case.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Johnson & Johnson printed a July 22, 2026 bullish call sweep for 290 contracts at the $230 strike expiring June 17, 2027, showing long-dated upside interest even as the chain also carried heavy bearish hedging.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Johnson & Johnson remains high quality and raised guidance, but the stock is near highs with limited upside and persistent talc litigation overhang.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: AVOID/SHORT CANDIDATE: Salesforce remains under direct AI pressure as KeyBanc downgraded the stock about two weeks ago, citing AI-driven disruption to the traditional seat-based SaaS model, with the shares down roughly 37% this year as investors question whether Agentforce can offset commoditization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce has cash flow and buybacks, but weak Agentforce feedback and AI pressure make the seat-based SaaS reset unresolved.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: A July 23, 2026 call sweep bought 1,000 Tesla contracts at the $350 strike expiring August 21, 2026, with 10,091 contracts of day volume and 525 volume/OI, which is fresh bullish positioning ahead of the next catalyst window.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Robotaxi and Optimus optionality is real, but valuation, profit miss, negative free cash flow, and accelerating AI capex make the risk/reward poor.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Albertsons is down 45% from its $20.91 52-week high to $11.44 after the July 23 earnings shock, but it still generated $217.4M of FY2025 net income and trades at 5.96x forward earnings with share repurchases still in the story; bear case is grocery-margin compression and leverage staying stubbornly high.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Albertsons is statistically cheap after the selloff, but the guidance cut, weak identical sales, narrow margins, and leverage look like value-trap conditions.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: UWM Holdings is 74% below its $7.14 high at $1.84, still profitable with $66.36M of net income and a 21.7% dividend yield, and it reports Aug. 5-6 after Morgan Stanley and Barclays both cut targets in July, making this a rate-sensitive capitulation setup; bear case is mortgage origination stays depressed if rates do not cooperate.
growth_picks.analyst_take: UWM's low multiple and high short interest are tempting, but elevated mortgage rates, negative FCF, leverage, and dividend risk make it too fragile.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: AVOID/SHORT CANDIDATE: Adobe has become a visible AI disruption victim, with Morgan Stanley cutting the stock to underweight on July 21, 2026 after citing the company's freemium shift, a roughly $500 million recurring-revenue hit, and heavier AI investment pressure on an already fragile software multiple.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe is optically cheap and profitable, but AI disruption, freemium transition risk, and leadership churn create unresolved ARR-quality questions.
